content.full_text:
/

                                                               7220/5


                                                        A-76-07-015


                              July 9, 1976


Paul H. Johnson, Esq.
Union Bank
Law Division
445 South Figueroa
Los Angeles CA  90071

Dear Mr. Johnson:

     This letter will serve to confirm our telephone conver-
sation of Thursday, July 8.  During that conversation, you
asked whether a loan made by a bank in the ordinary course
of business to a political candidate constituted a report-
able contribution.  I informed you that if the loan was a
legitimate loan supported by full and adequate consideration,
it would not be a contribution within the meaning of Gov.
Code Section 82015 and, therefore, would not be reportable.
I also informed you that such a transaction would not
constitute an expenditure since it would not involve a
communication which expressly advocates the election of
the candidate.  See Gov. Code Section 82025 and 2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18225.

     If you have any further questions, please do not
hesitate to contact me.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Kenneth Finney
                                        Chief
                                        Legal Division
KF:pvp


                                                                23

UNION BANK

JUL 23 1976

Headquarters Banking Office

445 South Figueroa Street, Post Office Box 2102, Los Angeles, California
Telephone (213) 687-

July 20, 1976

Mr. Kenneth Finney
Chief, Legal Division
State of California Fair Political
Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
1100 K Street Building
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Finney:

Thank you for your letter of July 9, 1976. As a follow up
to that letter and our subsequent conversation, it is my
understanding that the bank may lend money to candidates
and committees for political purposes. Also, in the event
that such loan is made in the ordinary course of business
and for fair and adequate consideration (i.e. no discount
given in interest rate charged or other special advantage)
that such loan need not be reported by the bank as contri-
bution or expenditure.

If this is correct, please acknowledge by signing and re-
turning the original of this letter. A copy is enclosed
for your files.

Thank you for your assistance on this matter.

Very truly yours,

Paul H. Johnson
Associate Counsel

PHJ/mi
Enclosure

Acknowledged [signature] Kenneth Finney

Dated: 2/29/76

SACRAMENTO ADDRESS
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO 95814
(916) 445-6490

POMONA OFFICE
1063 W. 6TH STREET
ONTARIO 91764
(714) 983-2757

POMONA OFFICE
505 S. GAREY AVENUE
POMONA 91767
(714) 622-1006

Assembly
California Legislature

COMMITTEES
AGRICULTURE
JUDICIARY
REVENUE AND TAXATION
VICE CHAIRMAN

SUBCOMMITTEE ON
FISCAL IMPACT LEGISLATION,
VICE CHAIRMAN

BILL McVITTIE
ASSEMBLYMAN, SIXTY-FIFTH DISTRICT
(POMONA AND WESTERN SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY)

July 28, 1976

Fair Political Practices
Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

RE: SACRAMENTO COFFEE FUND

Gentlemen:

This will confirm my telephone conversation with
your office today wherein we indicated that we would like to
set up a coffee fund for constituents and others who visit
my Sacramento legislative office.

Neither my secretary nor I wish to personally pay
for the coffee from this point forward since constituents
consume it in such large quantities.

Your office has indicated that my campaign fund,
"Friends of Assemblyman Bill McVittie", I.D. 746615, can issue
a check for $50 payable to Petty Cash and that this revolving
fund can be used for the purchase of coffee. We will maintain
receipts to support the continuing reimbursement to the fund
and the expenses will be properly reported on the committee
statements.

Thank you for answering the question so speedily.

NOTE: ADVICE GIVEN BY NAN HAMBLETON                    Sincerely,
-CONFIRMED
                                                       Bill McVittie
                                                       Assemblyman, 65th District


WJM:jf
cc: campaign committee file

sections.question_synthetic: These documents address multiple questions: (1) whether a bank loan to a political candidate in the ordinary course of business constitutes a reportable contribution, and (2) whether a campaign committee can use a revolving coffee fund paid for by constituents.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A legitimate bank loan with full and adequate consideration is not a reportable contribution or expenditure under Government Code Sections 82015 and 82025. A campaign committee may establish a revolving coffee fund with FPPC approval, funded by campaign committee checks.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple separate correspondence pieces: (1) FPPC response letter from Kenneth Finney to Paul Johnson dated July 9, 1976; (2) Johnson's confirmation letter dated July 20, 1976; (3) McVittie's request letter dated July 28, 1976 with advice noted as given by Nan Hambleton. No formal question/conclusion sections present; extracted from letter body text. Confidence reduced due to mixed document types and unclear formatting of McVittie letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: These documents address multiple questions: (1) whether a bank loan to a political candidate in the ordinary course of business constitutes a reportable contribution, and (2) whether a campaign committee can use a revolving coffee fund paid for by constituents.

CONCLUSION: A legitimate bank loan with full and adequate consideration is not a reportable contribution or expenditure under Government Code Sections 82015 and 82025. A campaign committee may establish a revolving coffee fund with FPPC approval, funded by campaign committee checks.

embedding.summary: This document contains correspondence between Union Bank's counsel and the FPPC regarding bank loans to political candidates, and a follow-up request from Assemblyman Bill McVittie about establishing a Sacramento office coffee fund, with FPPC confirmation that the campaign committee can fund it through a revolving account.
